{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "staged",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 0,
  "value": 0,

  "confidence": 0.95,

  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Mechanical seal face contamination from bare-hand touch introduces oils, moisture, and particulates that prevent proper sealing and accelerate face wear.",
    "Contaminated seal faces are likely to leak upon startup, causing immediate lubricant loss, bearing failure, or process fluid release."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Quarantine all contaminated seals immediately and do not install.",
    "Inspect staging area for additional contaminated components and review handling procedures.",
    "Obtain replacement seals and ensure they are handled only with clean, lint-free gloves or tools per SKF/Timken mounting guides.",
    "Retrain personnel on clean handling requirements for seal faces before resuming work."
  ],
  "rationale": "Touching a mechanical seal face with bare hands contaminates the lapped sealing surfaces with skin oils and dirt. Per SKF and general bearing/seal mounting best practices, seal faces must be handled only with clean, lint-free gloves or tools to maintain surface integrity. Installing a contaminated seal will lead to premature failure, leakage, and potential equipment damage. The condition as-staged is therefore non-compliant and must be rejected.",
  "references": [
    "SKF seal handling and mounting instructions: 'Never touch seal faces with bare hands; use clean, lint-free gloves.'",
    "Timken industrial seal installation guidelines: 'Contamination of seal faces is a leading cause of premature seal failure.'"
  ]
}
